ai_overview: Yesterday's activity centered on enhancing mobile user experience and finalizing core infrastructure, notably with the completion of the mobile bottom navigation bar and the global redesign of sign-in and registration modals. Significant progress was made on the Global Strategy Hub and the Dynamic Content API, both of which have transitioned into the QA phase alongside new brand offer term logic. You should follow up on the investigation into incorrect Ktag CT values on Gambling.com, as this poses a risk to data accuracy for operator pages. While there are no immediate blockers, keep an eye on the cross-team coordination required for the upcoming SlotsLaunch game grid and the international market alignment for non-English regions. Additionally, ensure the SEO cannibalization concerns raised during the UK casino bonus rewrite are fully addressed to protect existing traffic. High-level strategic alignment is also underway, with leadership finalizing H1 2026 OKRs that will soon dictate the team's broader roadmap.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Jira tickets or Slack posts found for 22 April.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Working on the Tinder-style offer match widget (GDCU-8546, In Progress) and initiated design for the new Genesis brand carousel component card (GDCU-8879, To Do). Focus is on enhancing user experience through interactive UI elements. Jira confirms both tickets updated 22 April.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Moving the 'Always On Betting Tips' page (GDCU-8512) into the product phase, aimed at enhancing consistent betting content delivery. Also active in #gdc-product-design-pod on 22 Apr, providing detailed guidance on logo formats for the NZ market headers — discussing optimal sizing and transparency requirements for circle and rectangle logo types. Activity upgraded from email's low assessment based on Slack and Jira evidence.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Participated in the non-English regions synchronization meeting and updated localization documentation. Also highly active in #gdc-product-led-growth on 22 Apr, posting multiple design guidance messages: guidance on offer card styling (italic text applied at elements level via Elements admin), container design standards for the new cool-tone background (white container with outline + shadow), and component naming conventions. Raised a query in #6amigos about creating a ticket for red-to-blue link colour changes sitewide. Activity upgraded from email's low assessment based on Slack activity.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Completed integration of full football coverage for Ace AI and finalized the mobile bottom navigation bar and image carousel shortcode for release. Also progressed logic for brand offer terms into the QA phase and documented user flow improvements for the UK slots page. Current focus is refining mobile casino hygiene and preparing for upcoming SlotsLaunch game grid development.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Finalized refinements for the New Global Strategy Hub and Sub Hub while overseeing implementation of fixes for the site container update, ensuring quality standards before moving to production. Supplementary: Active in #6amigos on 22 Apr sharing the list of tickets due for release on 23 Apr (11 tickets coordinated). Also active in #gdc-product-design-pod discussing NZ header logo format options.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Successfully completed the redesign and implementation of Sign In and Registration modals across all geographical regions (GDCU-8803, Done). This streamlines the user onboarding experience as part of a broader interface update. Jira confirms ticket moved to Done on 22 April. No current blockers noted.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: Viktor's email listed no activity, but Eoin was active in #gdc-pod on 22 April investigating content issues on the newly launched Iceland site. He identified that raw HTML added by editors was breaking pages and provided guidance to the content team on the fix required. Activity upgraded from email's none assessment based on Slack evidence.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Jira tickets updated on 22 April or Slack activity found.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: Finalized refinements for the Global Strategy Hub (GDCU-8823, Done) and resolved a bug affecting image carousel displays across site pages. Transitioned both the Dynamic Content API setup (GDCU-8807, In QA) and new brand offer term logic (GDCU-8862, In QA) into final testing. Jira confirms three tickets updated on 22 April.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Previously noted as being on annual leave in planning discussions (referenced by Gabriel Cornoiu in #gdc-pdmt on 13 Apr). No Jira or Slack activity found.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: Viktor's email listed no activity, but Jira shows GDCU-8836 (Create an Image Carousel Shortcode) moved to Done on 22 April. This is a meaningful delivery that was captured in Josh Smith's release planning but not attributed to Nebojsa in Viktor's email. Activity upgraded from email's none assessment based on Jira evidence.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: Finalized the mobile bottom navigation bar (GDCU-8627, Done) as part of the company's CRO efforts. Active in #gdc-pod on 22 Apr, alerting the team that two UK VWO tests needed to be paused before the next day's global UK release of the bottom nav menu to avoid test conflicts.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: Finalized the rollout of full football coverage for Ace AI tips (GDCU-8714, Done) and shifted focus toward investigating and fixing incorrect Ktag CT values appearing on Gambling.com operator pages (GDCU-8863, In Progress) to ensure data accuracy. Both tickets confirmed updated in Jira on 22 April.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Jira or Slack activity found for 22 April.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: Viktor's email listed no activity, but Shane was active in #gdc-pod on 22 April performing QA observations. He flagged a content issue on the newly launched Iceland crypto page (gambling.com/is/en/crypto) and raised a concern that ACE AI feedback reports were not recording data since the weekend. Both issues were reported to Mike Pett and Carlos Blauth. Activity upgraded from email's none assessment based on Slack evidence.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: Drafted the Core H1 2026 OKRs for the team's long-term roadmap and established a new performance report template for the Brand Team. Also provided editorial guidance on the 'Next Celtic Manager' news page to ensure formatting and readability standards. Priorities were split between high-level roadmapping and maintaining quality control over live site assets.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: Coordinating a rewrite of the online casino bonus page for the UK market with a specific focus on addressing SEO cannibalization concerns to prevent the updated content competing with existing high-traffic pages. Activity also included routine 1:1 management meetings and internal team communication.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: Finalized RotoWire launch preparations by fixing critical CTA issues and handing off automation workflows, while reviewing content quality for the Spotlight news agent. Currently auditing structured data improvements for gambling.com and managing DMCA tracking, with upcoming plans to set up user review systems for Spotlight Vegas.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Completed the setup of the Dynamic Content API integrating Cloudflare Workers and D1 with the GDC Admin Panel (GDCU-8807), with the project now moved into QA for final testing. Also active in #gdc-pod on 22 Apr coordinating priority on SignIn/SignUp and bottom nav CRO tickets ahead of release. Raised the CA/FR Oplist Redesign as an upcoming sprint item in #6amigos.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: Expanding the Greek market by updating content and wagering tables for roulette and blackjack pages, and integrating Iceland into the global homepage and GA4 reporting. Coordinated with technical teams to audit schema markup and evaluate search rankings for Plinko content across multiple regions. Also managed administrative tasks including access requests and Slack channel configurations (creator of the new #gdc-nonen-market-updates channel for daily industry updates).

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: Updating Core H1 2026 OKRs as part of long-term strategic planning and roadmap alignment. Activity was otherwise limited. Earlier in the week (17 Apr in #core-division-leads) he announced the Finance report as the single source of truth for revenue data, with the commercial report being discontinued. Also posted in #core-division reminding the team to review AI-generated content before posting, reinforcing the high-agency principle.
